cards.label: of Britain's grid is weather-dependent or imported right now — wind, solar and interconnectors that fall away together. The rest is firm: gas, nuclear, biomass.
Britain has built 50.4 GW of wind & solar. Right now the whole fleet is delivering 11.4 GW.
the gas fleet out-produces every wind farm in Britain right now — 14.3 GW of gas against 11.4 GW of wind.
Each column is one day's wind since 2016. The wind rarely blows above a fraction of its capacity.
Every half-hour of the last year: red where Britain leaned on weather and imports, pale where firm power carried demand.
in 2026 (so far), Britain's wind ran below a tenth of its installed capacity.
of capacity — the wind fleet's worst day on record, 2016-01-19.
the longest run wind stayed below 10% of capacity — 2016-06-03 to 2016-06-19.
An Electricity Margin Notice is in force in Britain.
